scenarios.opening_query: The restaurant fired me the day after the health inspection and claims it's 'performance.'
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm the one who called the health department about the walk-in cooler — the inspector told my manager someone reported it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I called the health department about the cooler — and the inspector let slip that someone reported it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the employee made the report preceding the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I never reported anyone — I refused to serve the recalled shellfish that night, and that's what set the manager off.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I never reported anyone — I refused to serve the recalled shellfish, and that set him off.

scenarios.opening_query: My company 'restructured' me out three weeks after I turned 62.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'restructure' moved my duties to a 28-year-old hired the month before — and the VP had asked HR about my 'retirement timeline' twice.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My duties went to a 28-year-old hired a month earlier — and the VP had twice asked HR about my 'retirement timeline.'
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came right after I told finance I wouldn't certify inventory numbers I knew were inflated.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came right after I refused to certify inflated inventory numbers.

scenarios.opening_query: I was fired by text message while I was in the hospital. Who does that?
scenarios.hidden_fact: The hospitalization was covered FMLA leave — approved paperwork on file — and the text came on day three of it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was approved FMLA leave — paperwork on file — and they fired me on day three of it.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I'd only been there four months — no FMLA eligibility — but the injury was from refusing to skip the machine lockout, which I'd reported that morning.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'd only been there four months, so no FMLA — but the injury came from refusing to skip lockout, and I'd reported it that morning.
scenarios.vetting_note: Eligibility fact moves the same outrage between statutes.

scenarios.opening_query: After I won our region's top sales award they suddenly 'discovered' problems with my expense reports.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'discovery' came two weeks after I reported the regional director for groping me at the awards dinner — HR has my written complaint.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It came two weeks after I reported the regional director for groping me at that dinner — HR has my complaint in writing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a harassment complaint preceded the retaliation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came two weeks after I told the director I'd be reporting our fake demo numbers to the client — and refused to present them again.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came after I refused to present the fake demo numbers again.

scenarios.opening_query: My warehouse cut me loose right before peak season with no explanation after five years of perfect attendance.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > Union Issues - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It happened the week our organizing cards went public — I'm on the organizing committee, and three of the five of us were cut together.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the week our union cards went public — I'm on the organizing committee, and three of five of us got cut together.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether union organizing preceded the terminations
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No organizing here — it came the day after I filed my second workers' comp claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No union stuff — it was the day after my second comp claim.
scenarios.vetting_note: Organizing-retaliation has its own route (NLRB) behind the same firing.

scenarios.opening_query: I was fired for my disability, plain and simple, and I want to hold them accountable.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee || Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Looking at the timeline honestly — they accommodated my disability for years; the firing came the week after I served jury duty they'd begged me to postpone.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Honestly, they accommodated me for years — the firing came the week after jury duty they'd begged me to postpone.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which protected activity the timeline actually supports
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The timeline is clean — fired eight days after my accommodation request, with the manager's 'we need able-bodied people' comment witnessed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Fired eight days after my accommodation request — with a witnessed 'we need able-bodied people' comment.
scenarios.vetting_note: User's own theory corrected by their timeline; strong answer-use test.

scenarios.opening_query: The nonprofit I gave a decade to eliminated my role a month after my big grant report went in.
scenarios.hidden_fact: My report flagged that the director had moved grant funds to cover the gala shortfall — the funder called; the elimination followed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My report flagged the director moving grant funds to the gala shortfall — the funder called, and then my job vanished.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether reporting fund misuse preceded the elimination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The report was routine — the elimination came after I refused to backdate volunteer hours the director wanted 'adjusted.'
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The report was routine — this came after I refused to backdate volunteer-hours records.
scenarios.vetting_note: Grant-misuse reporting in the nonprofit sector.

scenarios.opening_query: Fired from my tech job right before my options vested, with a 'confidential' reason no one will explain.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The exit packet in my inbox has a separation agreement, IP assignment, and a non-compete — with 21 days to sign; independent review of it is the immediate need.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's an exit packet — separation agreement, IP assignment, non-compete — with a 21-day clock. It needs independent review first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether reviewable exit documents are pending
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They offered nothing — no packet — and the timing right before vesting looks deliberate; I want the firing itself pursued.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No packet at all — and the pre-vesting timing looks deliberate. I want the firing pursued.

scenarios.opening_query: My boss found out about my second job and fired me for 'divided loyalty' even though I never missed a shift.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'second job' is my weekend work as a mosque youth leader — he called it 'that madrassa thing' in front of two coworkers.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'second job' is volunteering as a mosque youth leader — he called it 'that madrassa thing' in front of coworkers.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether religious activity drew the hostility
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The second job is bartending — and the firing came only after I filed for the overtime he owed me at the first job.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's bartending — and the firing came right after I filed for my unpaid overtime.

scenarios.opening_query: I got terminated while deployed with the National Guard and came home to no job.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Administrative Law > Military/Veterans
scenarios.hidden_fact: The JAG office says it's a USERRA case — reemployment rights for service members, with the Department of Labor's veterans unit as the first stop.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: JAG says it's USERRA — service-member reemployment rights, starting with the DOL veterans unit.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The Guard part they actually handled fine — the firing came after I got back and refused to falsify the delivery manifests.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They handled the Guard part fine — this came after I refused to falsify manifests once I was back.
scenarios.vetting_note: USERRA routing is a genuinely different remedial path.
